items.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: Infosys, Maruti among 5 stocks that could give 10-40% return
items.description: Brokerage commentary remains action-heavy, with a mix of upgrades and reaffirmed bullish calls across IT, auto, FMCG bottling, infrastructure, and specialty chemicalsWe have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources:

items.title: Positive Breakout: These 7 stocks cross above their 200 DMAs
items.description: In the NSE list of stocks with a market cap over Rs 10,000 crore, seven stocks saw their closing prices cross above their 200-day moving averages (DMA) on February 18, according to StockEdge.com's technical scan data. The 200-day daily moving average (DMA) is used as a key indicator by traders for determining the overall trend in a particular stock. As long as the stock price is above the 200-day SMA on the daily time frame, it is generally considered to be an overall uptrend. Take a look:

items.title: Marushika Technology shares to list today. Check GMP ahead of NSE SME debut
items.description: Marushika Technology is set to debut on the NSE SME platform on February 19, with a modest grey market premium suggesting a slight listing gain. The company, operating in IT and telecom infrastructure, including defence projects, raised Rs 26.97 crore through its IPO. Proceeds will fund working capital and debt repayment.

items.title: GIFT Nifty up 30 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
items.description: Indian equity markets continued their upward trend for a third consecutive session, signaling a gradual recovery. Analysts anticipate sustained market strength with a positive bias, influenced by global market sentiment and domestic sector-specific developments. The Nifty futures on Gift Nifty indicate a positive start to trading.

items.title: Stocks in news: Cochin Shipyard, Bharat Forge, HUL, Dr Reddy's, L&T
items.description: Indian markets saw modest gains for a third straight session. Key stocks like Cochin Shipyard and Bharat Forge are in focus. Cochin Shipyard secured new orders. Bharat Forge is exploring technology collaborations. Hindustan Unilever plans manufacturing expansion. Dr Reddy's acquired specialty brand trademarks. L&T announced a venture for AI factory infrastructure.

items.title: Metal stocks glitter on Dalal Street, eye stronger March quarter
items.description: Indian metal stocks are performing strongly. Prices for steel and non-ferrous metals have risen significantly. This is due to increased demand, government policies, and limited supply. Steel companies are seeing better earnings. Non-ferrous producers also benefit from global price trends and a weaker rupee. The current quarter is typically strong for metal volumes.

items.title: M&A funding norms open up a $15-b opportunity for banks
items.description: New Reserve Bank of India guidelines liberalize merger and acquisition financing, allowing Indian banks to fund up to 75% of acquisition costs with a 3:1 debt-equity ratio. This unlocks a potential $10-15 billion annual opportunity for domestic lenders, previously dominated by offshore entities, fostering a new, high-value asset class.

items.title: Unique transaction ID to be must for OTC derivatives deals: RBI
items.description: The Reserve Bank of India will mandate unique transaction identifiers (UTIs) for all over-the-counter derivative trades starting January 1, 2027. This move aims to enhance traceability and risk monitoring for instruments like FX and interest rate derivatives.  Market participants must upgrade systems for compliance, with CCIL expected to provide operational guidelines soon.
